snippet: A full time teacher will be in attendance for a minimum of 7 hours daily commencing no less than 10 minutes before the morning pupil instructional session, unless otherwise agreed between the principal and a teacher. In addition to the attendance requirements above, teachers may be required to undertake other duties for up to 3 hours, including attendance at meetings provided that not more than 2 hours of the 3 hours can be used for meetings. Any meetings held beyond the 7 hours of daily attendance will be adjacent to the 7 hours of daily attendance and not exceed 1 hour unless otherwise agreed using the consultative arrangements. When scheduling meetings and other duties, the principal should be cognisant of the workload implications. Parent-teacher meetings should be scheduled in a manner that not only provides the maximum opportunity for all parents to attend, but also takes into account the needs of the teachers concerned, especially those teachers employed on a part-time basis. A teacher is entitled to a paid lunch break of not less than 30 minutes free from assigned duties between the hours 11:30am to 2:30pm.
